talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial metabolomics of pancreas and breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the trial was never powered to give a definitive answer, right, but it had 16 valuable patients per arm randomized"
Speaker said: "When they were in ketosis, they were not in very deep ketosis, and there was a correlation between the extent of ketosis and longer survival in the trial"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Ketogenic diet + standard chemotherapy in metastatic pancreatic cancer
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Trial did not control for phytochemical differences between standard chow and ketogenic diet, confounding interpretation of efficacy
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients responded better when deeper ketosis was achieved

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Unnamed first‑line metastatic pancreatic cancer trial (ketogenic diet vs control)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Combination of carbohydrate‑restricted diet with gemcitabine/abraxane/cisplatin
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Small sample size (16 per arm) and lack of stratification for diet adherence
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No baseline or on‑treatment ketone measurements used for patient selection
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered to detect modest survival benefit; no pre‑specified statistical plan disclosed

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that glucose is depleted in cancer cells (spatial metabolomics) yet concluded that further glucose restriction via ketogenic diet could be therapeutically beneficial – ignoring the paradox that cancer cells already appear glucose‑starved

key_data_gaps: No overall survival hazard ratios reported
Lack of metabolic imaging correlates for diet adherence
No mechanistic biomarker linking ketone utilization to tumor response

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify patients with high intratumoral ketone utilization (e.g., CD8+ T‑cell 3‑hydroxybutyrate uptake) to enrich for responders to ketogenic diet‑based regimens
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "CD8 positive T cells within the tumor preferentially use 3‑hydroxybutyrate"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate spatial metabolomics with single‑cell transcriptomics to predict ketone‑dependent immune niches
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: NCTxxxxxx (ketogenic diet trial) for patient‑level ketone measurements
PubMed: studies on tumor ketone metabolism in pancreatic cancer

crispro_opportunity.description: Redesign the pancreatic cancer diet trial to control for phytochemical content and include prospective ketone monitoring as a stratification biomarker
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that phytochemical differences between chow and ketogenic diet affected drug metabolism
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model drug‑diet interactions and simulate pharmacokinetic impacts of diet‑derived phytochemicals
external_validation_needed: Pharmacokinetic data on gemcitabine metabolism with/without phytochemicals

cited_competitors.name: Virta Health
cited_competitors.context: provided electronic monitoring of patient ketosis in the diet trial

cited_competitors.name: Greg Dougherty's lab
cited_competitors.context: previous high‑profile papers claimed immunosuppressive cells love lactate

unresolved_questions: Does deeper ketosis (higher blood β‑hydroxybutyrate) consistently improve survival across larger patient cohorts?
What is the relative contribution of diet‑induced changes in drug metabolism versus metabolic stress on tumor control?

assets_to_track: Ketogenic diet adjuncts for pancreatic cancer

talk_metadata.talk_title: Metabolic regulation of cancer cell growth via mitochondrial NADPH
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We observed that loss of NDK2 completely disrupted proline biosynthesis"
Speaker said: "We see a reduction in lipoic acid in all of these proteins, indicating that NADK2 loss affects general protein lipoylation"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory pathways for mitochondrial NADPH (e.g., cytosolic NADK retargeted to mitochondria) not fully explored
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Proline supplementation fully rescues proliferation of NDK2‑KO cells

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker showed that catalytic‑inactive NADK2 cannot restore lipoylation, indicating the enzymatic activity is essential and may be targetable
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on proline supplementation to rescue NDK2 loss suggests a narrow therapeutic window if targeting NDK2 alone

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that mitochondrial NADPH supports TCA cycle flux via PDH lipoylation, yet concluded that the main function of mitochondrial NADPH is proline synthesis – overlooking the broader metabolic impact

key_data_gaps: In vivo validation of NDK2 inhibition in tumor models
Assessment of toxicity in normal proliferative tissues

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the proline auxotrophy induced by NDK2 loss with proline‑degrading enzymes or transport inhibitors to prevent rescue of tumor cells
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Proline alone was able to restore cell proliferation in these proline auxotroph NDK2 knockout cells"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict metabolic flux rerouting upon NDK2 inhibition and identify synthetic lethal partners in proline metabolism
external_validation_needed: DepMap dependency data for PRODH/PRODH2 in NDK2‑amplified cancers
In vivo efficacy of proline transport inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO's ability to model rare post‑translational modifications (lipoylation) to discover biomarkers of mitochondrial NADPH deficiency
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Loss of NADK2 shows a reduction in lipoic acid in all of these proteins"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate proteomics‑derived lipoylation status with metabolic modeling to stratify tumors dependent on mitochondrial NADPH
external_validation_needed: Mass‑spec datasets of protein lipoylation in patient tumors
Correlation of NDK2 expression with lipoylation signatures

cited_competitors.name: Ralph DeBerardinis
cited_competitors.context: cited for lactate utilization data in lung cancer

unresolved_questions: Does mitochondrial NADPH affect mitochondrial translation through mechanisms beyond fatty‑acid synthesis?
What is the impact of NDK2 inhibition on normal stem cell compartments (bone marrow, intestine) in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Methodological insights into whole‑body metabolism and nucleotide constraints in cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you simply take the folate level in MPM and make it look like RPMI, now they don't care about nucleotide salvage at all"
Speaker said: "Standard mouse chow is actually high in folates because we breed the mice"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Low extracellular folate in physiological plasma‑like media
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Folates are the limiting cofactor for de novo nucleotide synthesis; standard culture media overestimates folate availability
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Cells grown in mouse plasma‑like media survive only when salvage pathways are intact

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that folate depletion dramatically slows B‑ALL growth, yet suggested that standard culture conditions are “physiological” for nucleotide metabolism – ignoring the mismatch

key_data_gaps: Direct in vivo validation that folate limitation drives replication stress in tumors
Effect of ketogenic diet on systemic folate levels

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a folate‑status biomarker panel to identify tumors reliant on salvage pathways for nucleotide synthesis
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If you take the folate level in MPM and make it look like RPMI, now they don't care about nucleotide salvage at all"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate plasma metabolomics with tumor gene expression to predict folate‑dependent vulnerabilities
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: studies measuring plasma folate in leukemia patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine antifolate agents with drugs that exacerbate replication stress (e.g., gemcitabine) in low‑folate microenvironments
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that low folate leads to DNA replication stress which could sensitize cells to chemotherapy
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate synergistic effects of folate depletion and nucleoside analogs on DNA synthesis flux
external_validation_needed: Preclinical trials of antifolates + gemcitabine in mouse plasma‑like media models
NCT numbers for antifolate‑based combination studies

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate tissue‑specific nutrient profiling (e.g., folate, lactate) into cancer trial eligibility criteria
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that each tissue has a distinct nutrient environment that shapes metabolic phenotypes
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict which tumor types will be most sensitive to nutrient‑modulating interventions based on tissue‑specific metabolite maps
external_validation_needed: Metabolomics datasets of human tumor interstitial fluid
Regulatory guidance on biomarker‑driven trial designs

cited_competitors.name: Greg Dougherty's lab
cited_competitors.context: previous lactate utilization studies in Tregs

cited_competitors.name: Ralph DeBerardinis
cited_competitors.context: cited for lung cancer lactate tracing data

unresolved_questions: How does systemic folate supplementation affect tumor replication stress and response to chemotherapy in vivo?
Do ketogenic diets alter circulating folate or other one‑carbon metabolites in patients?

assets_to_track: Ketogenic diet clinical trials in pancreatic cancer
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing plasma‑like media platforms for drug screening
